title: rosa sepium subsp. agrestis
intro: Rosa sepium subsp. agrestis, also known as wild briar or dog rose, is a subspecies of rose plant that is native to Europe and Western Asia. It has been used for centuries for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional herbal remedies, due to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Rosa sepium subsp. agrestis include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce the risk of chronic diseases such as heart disease and certain types of cancer. Rosa sepium subsp. agrestis can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to promote overall health and well-being.
